Our business operates on the Joint Liability Group model to offer collateral free micro-credit to economically active women in rural and semi-urban areas, thereby expanding access to financial services.

Our clients are predominantly economically active women (99%) from rural and semi-urban areas, belonging to low-income households and engaged in income-generating activities. They represent a segment often underserved by traditional banks and other financial channels. We serve over 3 Million clients across 27 states, reflecting our strong commitment to inclusive financial access and the empowerment of marginalized communities
All outstanding complaints logged in the last week of the financial year were subsequently resolved by the first week of April 2025, resulting in zero pending cases to date.

Financing has an important role to play in enabling businesses and economies to transition to a sustainable economy. 
The NBFC-MFI sector in India is one of the key drivers of the economy and significantly contributes to the country’s GDP as well as employment. As a financial services company, we recognize that directing capital towards such small businesses presents a substantial opportunity for us to make a meaningful impact. We firmly believe that achieving financial success should be intricately linked with our contribution to the greater good of society.

Being an NBFC-MFI, focusing on providing loans to income generating businesses in the rural and semi-urban areas that are underserved, we are addressing a major financing gap, thus, enabling such businesses and entrepreneurs to obtain loans to scale up their operations, improve efficiency and for other business expansion related purposes. Our efforts involve placing a heightened focus on providing loans to women entrepreneurs with a focus on sustainability and inclusive
growth.

Under penetration and untapped market presents a huge opportunity for growth for NBFC-MFIs. Millions of individuals, especially in rural and semi-urban areas, remain excluded from the formal credit system. This credit gap results from a lack of access to traditional banking services, limited credit history, and various other socio-economic factors, etc.
With increasing focus of the government towards financial inclusion, rising financial awareness, and increasing smartphone and internet penetration, there is a significant potential for expanding the reach of NBFC-MFIs.

We have presence in 27 states and union territories. To adapt to and mitigate risks while tapping into underpenetrated markets, we implement comprehensive risk
management strategies. These strategies include developing robust credit scoring models using alternative data sources, leveraging advanced technologies for predictive analytics and fraud detection, and diversifying our portfolio across regions and sectors. Establishing strong governance and compliance practices, along
with regular audits, ensures operational stability and adherence to regulations. We invest in staff training and educate our clients on responsible borrowing to further reduce default risks.

A skewed asset-liability profile could lead to a liquidity crunch, resulting in significantly higher funding costs. The company has a diversified funding profile in terms of products, institutions, and countries, with a base of 76 lenders comprising a healthy mix of banks, non-banks, and global impact lenders.

The Company employs prudent asset-liquidity management and maintains a well-diversified liability profile to minimize market turbulence impacts. 
We continuously monitor market liquidity conditions.

Customer experience is a significant opportunity to enhance our competitiveness and build lasting relationships with our clients. By delivering exceptional services and products, we can increase customer satisfaction, retention, and loyalty.
Given that the company provides financial products and services to millions of customers, any undesirable customer experience could result in loss of customers or
even reputational loss.

To maximize the opportunity of customer experience, we have adopted the following approach:
Digital Innovation: Embracing new age, cutting edge technologies and leveraging innovative technologies through our “PhyGital” model which uses technology and authentic in person service to create loans which support the ambitions of our customers. We engage directly with our clients and work through loan applications together, in person, at the customer’s pace.
Grievance Redressal Mechanisms: We have a dedicated grievance redressal mechanism “Sparsh” to promptly address customer related complaints and grievances through multiple channels. Each complaint is thoroughly reviewed, monitored, and resolved to achieve complete customer satisfaction. We monitor customer queries and grievances on various social media channels and provide quick resolution.

Dependence on technology platforms and associated vendors heightens the system’s vulnerability. In the event of a security breach, sensitive customer information could be exposed, leading to regulatory fines, legal liabilities, loss of customer trust, and damage to the company’s brand image. Ensuring robust data protection measures is essential to safeguard the interests of both - the institution and its client

We have enhanced our IT infrastructure and Information Security team to effectively monitor and address these challenges on an ongoing basis. We have a welldefined framework and policy on cyber security and data privacy. This policy assures users that all personal information provided through the website is treated with utmost confidentiality and is not shared with third parties in most circumstances, unless explicitly solicited and permitted by the user.

At SCNL, governance is grounded in transparency, accountability, and future-readiness—strengthened in FY 2024-25 through 100% independent committees, enhanced Anti-Bribery and Corruption safeguards, a 94% average board attendance across nine meetings, and a grievance redressal system resolving 99% of complaints within defined timelines. For us governance is not a box-ticking exercise but the cornerstone of stakeholder trust—rooted in responsibility that extends beyond compliance to environmental stewardship, with initiatives like solar-powered infrastructure, smart energy systems, and clean energy programs reducing 2.1 lakh tonnes of CO2, alongside continuous GHG assessments, sustainable operational practices, and recognitions like the SQS2 rating from Moody’s and a ‘Top Quartile’ ESG rating.

The objective of the Anti-Bribery & Anti-Corruption Policy is to uphold and promote lawful business practices across the organization while preventing and prohibiting corruption, bribery, and related misconduct. The Audit Committee oversees policy compliance, while the HR Department is responsible for reporting any issues or concerns. The Company has implemented various codes and policies to effectively handle conflicts of interest involving members of the Board. SCNL requires all the Board members to submit a mandatory declaration, ensuring their compliance with the Code of Conduct. This requirement applies to all directors and senior management and employees across the group. By adhering to these standards, we aim to uphold and enforce proper business conduct. The Code serves as a deterrent  against misconduct and promotes ethical behaviour within the organization.

In case, if there is any interest of any Directors then he/she is not allowed to participate in that particular proposal. Further, the Company also  has a policy on Materiality of Related Party Transactions and Dealing with Related Party Transactions to take care of Related Party Transactions which can present a potential or actual conflict of interest which may be against the best interest of the Company and its shareholders. The said Policy is available on the website of the Company at https://satincreditcare.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/RPT-Policy.pdf.

SCNL ensures transparent communication and access to relevant information for stakeholders, in line with regulatory requirements and to the extent necessary, while maintaining the confidentiality of competitive plans and sensitive data.

The Company’s business activities include a constant process of engagement with stakeholders. This engagement is generally managed by the relevant business functions, with top management and senior executives also participating in discussions as required.

Any material feedback received from stakeholders is communicated to the Board of Directors on an ongoing basis. The Board is kept informed of developments arising from such engagement and offers its guidance and input on these matters. Through various committees, the Board also receives regular updates on stakeholder feedback.

Constant interaction with stakeholders enhances the Company’s ability to serve them better. Accordingly, the Company remains open to acting upon and incorporating relevant suggestions. Over the past few years, it has strengthened its business responsibility reporting and initiated several programs in response to stakeholder feedback.

For instance, based on key stakeholder input, the Company has implemented a Diversity & Inclusion Policy, a Workers’ Accommodation Policy, and installed fire extinguishers in most of its branches. Additionally, at stakeholder suggestion, the Company has calculated its GHG emissions and is currently in the process of conducting a human rights assessment.

The employee grievance redressal mechanism, Atoot Bandhan is designed for swift resolution and effective communication. Employees can voice their grievances by calling a toll-free number or emailing, ensuring accessibility and ease of reporting. These grievances are promptly addressed in real-time or formally registered and escalated to the relevant department for further action. Feedback and suggestions gathered during the redressal process are systematically shared with department heads, fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
 
The grievance redressal committee operates with the primary objective of resolving disputes within an average of 8 to 10 days of timeframe. If an employee remains dissatisfied with the decision, provisions allow for appeals to be made to the employer within one month. Regularly prepared reports, presented to the senior management and the board, serve to enhance grievance redressal practices and bolster employee satisfaction. This proactive approach underscores the Company's commitment to fostering a harmonious work environment where grievances are swiftly and fairly addressed.
Note - In both years, the cases reported by the Company were appropriately addressed.

The Company is committed to providing equal opportunities to all individuals and does not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on race, sex, nationality, ethnicity, origin, religion, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or language, as protected by applicable laws.

The Diversity & Inclusion Policy, Workplace Health & Safety Policy, Leave Policy, Benefits & Entitlements Policy, and Recruitment Policy each contain specific clauses addressing various forms of discrimination, reinforcing the Company's commitment to maintaining a safe, inclusive, and respectful working environment.

The Company has a written policy in place to protect its employees against sexual harassment at work. It is in compliance with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition, and Redressal) Act, 2013, and, on August 14, 2017, adopted a revised policy on Sexual Harassment (which is part of the Company’s HR Policy) to prohibit, prevent, and deter any acts of sexual harassment at work.

Reinforcing our ongoing commitment to an effective and POSH-compliant redressal mechanism, the members of the Internal Committee were reconstituted in March 2024 to ensure continued representation and alignment with statutory requirements.

The Company has a Board-approved Business Continuity Plan (BCP) in place. This plan is essential for ensuring the Company’s resilience against disruptions. It outlines procedures to maintain operations during unforeseen events such as natural disasters, cyber-attacks, or pandemics. The BCP includes risk assessments, recovery strategies, and communication protocols, with a focus on key components such as data backup, alternate work sites, and emergency contacts. Regular testing and updates ensure the plan remains effective and relevant.

The Company maintains two availability zones (AZs) to house its data, ensuring seamless operations during disruptions. Key elements of the plan include data redundancy, automated failover, and regular backups across both AZs. The BCP incorporates real-time data replication to maintain consistency and minimize data loss. Regular testing of disaster recovery protocols ensures operational readiness. Clear communication channels and defined roles for staff during incidents further support resilience. By leveraging the geographic separation of AZs, the Company mitigates risks from localized events and ensures the continuity of critical services for clients with minimal downtime.
Given the nature of the business, there has been no adverse impact to the environment.

In the year under reporting, the Company got an impact assessment of the business operations and its outcomes in the lives of the clients we serve by an external agency, 60_decibels. The impact assessment survey provided detailed results on our performance across the six dimensions of financial inclusion: Access, Business Impact, Household Impact, Client Protection, Resilience and Agency. Additionally, insights on client satisfaction, loan usage and challenges were provided that the Company finds actionable to improve its products and services.

During the reporting year, the Company did not develop any new intellectual property. However, it possesses complete inhouse technology. SCNL embarked on a transformative journey in the fiscal year 2016-17 by developing its own Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system.

This decision was driven by the limitations of the legacy application (BIJLI) the Company previously utilized, which lacked scalability due to constraints in its architecture. The in-house ERP development has
yielded significant advancements like transition to centralized architecture from decenterlized, real time data processing, geo fencing based solutions and AI and MI Integration.

The Company has established a structured grievance redressal mechanism to address customer complaints promptly and effectively, based on their priority. Multiple channels are available to ensure accessibility and convenience for customers when lodging their concerns:

Toll-Free Number: Customers can register their complaints by calling the toll-free number, without incurring any charges.

Center Meeting Register: Each service center maintains a dedicated section within the meeting register for customers to formally document their complaints.

Complaint Registers and Boxes: Strategically placed at branches and regional offices, these provide an offline method for customers to submit complaints.

Direct Escalation to Head Office: Customers can escalate unresolved issues directly to the Corporate  Office by:
-Sending a formal letter addressed to the Grievance Redressal Officer
-Calling the dedicated landline number
-Emailing: clientgrievance@satincreditcare.com

These channels reflect the Company’s commitment to responsive service and building customer trust. All complaints are systematically logged and communicated to both regional and corporate office operations teams. Additionally, internal audits are conducted regularly to assess customer satisfaction and ensure service quality.

To ensure customers fully understand and effectively utilize loan products suited to their lifecycle needs, the Company provides comprehensive training and support during the onboarding process. This includes a mandatory two-day group training session focused on responsible product usage and financial awareness.

Following the training, customers participate in a group recognition test and engage directly with the branch manager to reinforce key concepts and address any questions. At the time of loan disbursement, the branch manager further emphasizes the importance of credit discipline and responsible borrowing practices.
Customers are informed of any potential disruptions or discontinuation of essential services both during center meetings and via SMS to ensure timely awareness and minimal inconvenience.

the Company fully adheres to display regulations, ensuring complete transparency across all loan products. All terms and conditions are clearly outlined in the loan acknowledgement receipt, sanction letter, and loan card provided to customers. Additionally, information on various loan products and their ticket sizes is prominently displayed at all branch offices and published on the Company’s website.

The loan card contains detailed information including the rate of interest, loan tenure, repayment cycle and installment amount, processing fees, insurance coverage, and other applicable terms and conditions ensuring that customers are fully informed before proceeding.

Additionally, the Company fully adheres to display regulations, ensuring complete transparency across all loan products. All terms and conditions are clearly outlined in the loan acknowledgement receipt, sanction letter, Key Fact Statement (KFS), and loan card provided to customers. Additionally, information on various loan products and details is prominently displayed at all branch offices and published on the Company’s website. Furthermore, the Company ensures customers are fully informed of the total cost of borrowing through the disclosure of the Annual Percentage Rate (APR), which reflects not just the interest rate but also all associated fees and charges, presented in a consistent annualized format. The entity does display product information over and above what is mandated by local laws, providing enhanced clarity to customers.
